name: Cherokee Strip
description_short: Cherokee Strip Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative chartered in 1933, offering loans, deposits, and investment services to eligible members.
description_long:
Cherokee Strip Credit Union (CSCU) was originally founded in October 1933 as the Conoco Employees Credit Union in Ponca City, Oklahoma—only the second credit union chartered in the state. The organization operated under that name for 61 years before rebranding to Cherokee Strip Credit Union in July 1994. As a credit union, it operates as a non-profit cooperative owned entirely by its members, with no outside stockholders.

CSCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including consumer loans, mortgage loans, HELOCs, auto loans, credit cards, savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and investment services. The institution also provides insurance products and educational resources. According to their website, they feature competitive rates: HELOCs as low as 6.75%, auto loans as low as 5.75%, and credit cards as low as 9.99%. Members can access online banking, mobile app services, wire transfers, ACH e-alerts, and multiple physical branch locations.

CSCU distinguishes itself through its member-governance structure where a volunteer Board of Directors—elected annually by members—sets policy without compensation. As a cooperative, any profits beyond operational costs and required reserves are returned to members as monthly or special dividends on deposits. Member deposits are insured up to $250,000 through the National Credit Union Association (NCUA), providing federal protection similar to FDIC insurance at banks.

CSCU is best suited for individuals and families seeking traditional banking services who value member ownership, democratic governance, and the philosophy of community-focused finance. The organization maintains active community involvement through scholarship programs and financial education initiatives. However, as with most credit unions, membership eligibility requirements apply, and the institution's service area may be geographically limited.
pros: Member-owned non-profit structure means profits return to members as dividends rather than enriching external shareholders
NCUA deposit insurance protection up to $250,000 for member accounts
Volunteer Board of Directors elected by members ensures governance focused on member interests, not Wall Street profits
Competitive rates advertised: HELOCs as low as 6.75%, auto loans as low as 5.75%, credit cards as low as 9.99%
Comprehensive product suite including consumer loans, mortgages, HELOCs, auto loans, credit cards, deposits, IRAs, and investments
Mobile app and online banking with ACH e-alert notifications for deposits and withdrawals
Community commitment demonstrated through scholarship programs and financial education resources
cons: Membership eligibility requirements apply—not all consumers can join (typically requires living or working in service area or employer affiliation)
Website lacks specific branch locations, hours, or service area details making membership eligibility verification difficult
No information provided about specific APR ranges, fees, or full terms for advertised products—rates shown are promotional minimums only
Limited visibility into whether service is available nationwide or restricted to Oklahoma region based on historical Ponca City origins
best_for: Employees and families of Conoco or related organizations in the Ponca City, Oklahoma area
Members seeking community-focused banking with democratic governance and profit-sharing structure
Consumers in eligible service areas looking for competitive rates on mortgages, auto loans, and HELOCs
Members prioritizing member-owned financial institutions over for-profit bank alternatives
diagnosis: Cherokee Strip Credit Union is ideal for individuals in their service area seeking traditional banking services with member ownership benefits and competitive rates, particularly those with employer or geographic ties to the organization. The primary caveat is verifying membership eligibility and service area availability before applying, as credit union membership is restricted and not available to all consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Cherokee Strip — Credit Union in Ponca City, OK | CreditDoc
meta_description: Cherokee Strip — NCUA-insured credit union in Ponca City, OK. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
